hooks.command: echo '[Claude Code] Auto-compacting - context window full'

echo '## Post-Compact Context Restore

### Coding Rules
- Validation: npm run validate (frontend), pytest (backend)
- Files must stay under 500 lines
- Types go in types/index.ts
- developer owns code, tech-writer owns docs
- Always Read before Edit
- Max 200 lines per Edit operation

### CRITICAL ROUTING RULES
- NEVER use explore or general-purpose subagent types
- ALWAYS delegate via Task(subagent_type="agent-name", prompt="...")
- Available agents: architect, developer, debugger, reviewer, performance-optimizer, tech-writer, devops, researcher
- Routing: Bug->debugger, Code/features/tests->developer, Architecture/complex/planning->architect, Docs->tech-writer, Review/security->reviewer, CI/CD->devops, DB/perf->performance-optimizer, Research->researcher
- MANDATORY: Auto-route every message. No confirmation. Agent-first. NEVER edit code directly.
'

echo '## Claude Scaffolding Protocol

### BLOCKED SUBAGENT TYPES
- NEVER use general-purpose subagent -- conflicts with custom agents
- NEVER use explore for planning/analysis -- only for quick file searches
- plan (planning mode) is allowed

### MANDATORY BEHAVIOR
1. Auto-route -- every message is a task, route to agent immediately
2. No confirmation -- do not ask, just delegate
3. Concise responses -- short status, no verbose explanations unless asked
4. Agent-first -- NEVER edit code/docs directly, ALWAYS delegate

Response format: [Agent: name] Task -> Result (1-2 lines)

### AGENTS (11)
Route via: Task(subagent_type="agent-name", prompt="...")

- analyst       -- ambiguous requests, requirements, scope, feasibility, proposals
- architect     -- system design, API design, implementation planning, multi-file refactoring
- researcher    -- new API integration, library questions, best practices (gate: score >= 80)
- developer     -- implementation, bug fixes, features, tests, UI/styling (gate: validation passes)
- debugger      -- bug reports, unexpected behavior, errors
- reviewer      -- after code changes, security analysis, threat modeling (gate: no criticals)
- performance-optimizer -- performance issues, database design, schema, migrations, queries
- tech-writer   -- documentation, CHANGELOG updates
- devops        -- CI/CD, deployment, infrastructure
- gitops        -- branch management, conflict resolution, git history, push to remote
- coordinator   -- analyzes tasks, decomposes into agent step sequences

### DECISION TREE
NEVER answer directly. ALWAYS delegate.

- Bug fix -> debugger -> developer
- Complex feature -> analyst -> architect -> developer
- Simple feature / tests / UI -> developer
- Code/implementation question -> developer
- Architecture question -> architect
- Requirements / scope / feasibility -> analyst
- Docs / usage / library -> researcher -> tech-writer
- Planning / ambiguous -> analyst
- Review / security -> reviewer
- CI/CD -> devops
- Database / performance -> performance-optimizer
- Git operations / commit / merge / push -> gitops
- After ANY worktree agent completes -> gitops (commit + merge + push)
- Multi-agent coordination -> coordinator
- Default -> analyst

### DELEGATION FORMAT
Task(
    subagent_type="developer",
    prompt="Description of what to implement",
    description="Short label"
)

### KEY RULES
- Files < 500 lines -- refactor if approaching limit
- Validate before commit -- npm run validate (frontend) / pytest (backend)
- tech-writer owns docs -- only tech-writer modifies README/CHANGELOG
- developer owns code -- only developer modifies source files
- Max 200 lines per Edit operation
'

echo '## Claude Scaffolding Protocol (Resumed Session)

### BLOCKED SUBAGENT TYPES
- NEVER use general-purpose subagent -- conflicts with custom agents
- NEVER use explore for planning/analysis -- only for quick file searches

### MANDATORY BEHAVIOR
1. Auto-route -- every message is a task, route to agent immediately
2. No confirmation -- do not ask, just delegate
3. Agent-first -- NEVER edit code/docs directly, ALWAYS delegate

### AGENTS (11)
Task(subagent_type="agent-name", prompt="...")

- analyst, architect, researcher, developer, debugger, reviewer
- performance-optimizer, tech-writer, devops, gitops, coordinator

### DECISION TREE
- Bug fix -> debugger -> developer
- Complex feature -> analyst -> architect -> developer
- Simple feature / tests / UI -> developer
- Architecture question -> architect
- Docs -> researcher -> tech-writer
- Review / security -> reviewer
- CI/CD -> devops
- DB / performance -> performance-optimizer
- Git / commit / push -> gitops
- After worktree agent -> gitops (commit + merge + push)
- Multi-agent coordination -> coordinator
- Default -> analyst

NEVER answer directly. ALWAYS delegate.
'

description: Claude Code settings for (project) - AUTONOMOUS MODE

agents.description: Agent configuration (hooks trigger suggestions, not auto-execution)
